title: Aciotis indecora
taxonomy: Aciotis indecora is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The plant is native to South America, specifically in the subtropical regions of Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay. It belongs to the genus Aciotis, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Americas. The classification of Aciotis indecora is further supported by its botanical characteristics, such as its leaves and flowers. The plant is also known to be a part of the larger family of Melastomataceae, which comprises over 1,400 species of flowering plants.
